pmf:baivP1CustomerDiscovery.schema:name: BAIV P1 Customer Discovery and Data Enrichment Framework
pmf:baivP1CustomerDiscovery.schema:description: Specialized customer discovery approach focusing on data enrichment, market problems, customer questions, and workflow pain points
pmf:focus: Market and Problems to Solve identification
Workflows that cause grief or have greatest potential if solved

dataEnrichment.description: Systematic collection and analysis of customer data to understand behavior patterns, pain points, and workflow inefficiencies
activities: Customer data mining and pattern analysis
Problem severity scoring and correlation analysis

marketProblemIdentification.description: Deep dive into market-specific problems that represent the sharpest pain points
approach: Map customer workflows and identify friction points
Categorize problems by severity and frequency
Identify workflow bottlenecks with highest impact
Assess problems that cause most customer grief

customerQuestionAnalysis.description: Systematic analysis of questions customers ask to identify underlying problems and education needs
framework: Frequency analysis of support tickets and queries
Mapping questions to underlying workflow problems

workflowGriefAssessment.description: Identification of workflows causing greatest customer grief or representing highest potential for improvement

pmf:deliverables: Customer Data Enrichment Report
Market Problem Hierarchy (sharpest problems identified)
Customer Question Analysis and FAQ Optimization
Workflow Grief Assessment with improvement potential
3X-5X Opportunity Map for highest-impact workflows
pmf:baivP1CustomerDiscovery.pmf:integration: Feeds directly into Customer Discovery (1.1) Key Questions Framework and Market Analysis (1.3) processes

@graph.rdfs:label: BAIV MVP1.2 PMF Process Ontology
@graph.rdfs:comment: Comprehensive Product-Market Fit process ontology for continuous assessment and product development optimization
@graph.schema:creator: BAIV Product Innovation Team
@graph.pmf:purpose: Enable systematic PMF achievement for startups and enterprises through measurable, repeatable processes

@graph.rdfs:label: Product-Market Fit
@graph.rdfs:comment: The degree to which a product satisfies strong market demand, measured by customer retention, satisfaction, and willingness to pay premium pricing
@graph.pmf:definition: Product-Market Fit (PMF) occurs when a product delivers significant value to a clearly defined market segment, evidenced by sustainable customer acquisition, high retention rates (>40% monthly for SaaS), strong user engagement, and customers' willingness to pay premium pricing for the solution.

pmf:measurementCriteria.ellisScore: >=w% customers very disappointed if product unavailable

pmf:benefits: Sustainable competitive advantage through customer loyalty
Scalable customer acquisition with predictable unit economics
Premium pricing power due to unique value delivery
Reduced customer acquisition costs through word-of-mouth
Accelerated product development through clear customer feedback

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Process
@graph.rdfs:comment: Systematic methodology for achieving and maintaining product-market fit

pmf:processFeatures.datadriven: Quantitative metrics and qualitative insights integration
pmf:processFeatures.customerCentric: Direct customer feedback drives all decisions
pmf:processFeatures.crossFunctional: Marketing, Product, Sales, and Customer Success alignment
pmf:processFeatures.scalable: Framework applicable from startup to enterprise levels
pmf:processFeatures.aiEnhanced: Machine learning optimization and predictive analytics

@graph.rdfs:label: Customer Discovery
@graph.rdfs:comment: Systematic identification and analysis of target customer segments using structured heuristic framework

pmf:heuristicRules.rdfs:label: Three Layer Market Winner Framework
pmf:heuristicRules.rdfs:comment: The winner in any market is the organization that successfully executes on 3 layers

pmf:layer1.name: The Business Layer
pmf:layer1.description: Customer Experience (CX) and effective Monetization Model that turns Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) into profits

pmf:layer2.name: The Solution Layer
pmf:layer2.description: How the solution as a software product (form and function) solves real world customer problems or augments existing workflows with minimal friction

pmf:layer3.name: The Technology Layer
pmf:layer3.description: Software, hardware and infrastructure that forms the core of the organization's reason for being and modus operandi

pmf:improvementThreshold.description: Improvements and benefits to customer experience must be at least 3-5x better than current solutions to justify switch costs
pmf:improvementThreshold.rationale: Customers will only switch if the new solution is significantly better to overcome time, effort, and risk of switching from existing workflow
pmf:improvementThreshold.positioning: Position as 'can't live without it' solution

pmf:prioritizationFramework.rdfs:label: Sharpest Problems First Prioritization
pmf:prioritizationFramework.description: Focus roadmap on solving the biggest, most beneficial problems first as these have highest correlation to success

pmf:prioritizationMatrix.yAxis: Problem Severity (Low Impact → Really Sharp Big Problem)

pmf:latentDemand.definition: Customer need that exists but customers may not be fully aware of the problem or why it matters most
pmf:latentDemand.approach: Need to educate not just about the problem but why solving it matters most
pmf:latentDemand.strategy: Focus on what matters most - bottom line impact and workflow transformation

pmf:activities: Market segmentation analysis
Customer persona development
Pain point identification and severity ranking
Buying behavior analysis
Competitive landscape mapping
Workflow analysis and friction assessment
3X-5X improvement opportunity identification
pmf:deliverables: Ideal Customer Profile (ICP)
Customer Journey Maps
Sharpest Problems Hierarchy
Competitive Analysis Report
Market Size Assessment
3X-5X Improvement Opportunities Map
Prioritization Matrix (2x2)
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: >=n validated customer interviews per segment with sharpest problems identified and quantified

@graph.rdfs:label: Customer Discovery Sub-Processes
@graph.rdfs:comment: Detailed sub-processes for systematic customer discovery execution

@graph.rdfs:label: Key Questions to Ask You and Your Team (1.1)
@graph.rdfs:comment: Structured question framework for internal team alignment before customer research

@graph.rdfs:label: Market Analysis Questions (1.3)
@graph.rdfs:comment: Systematic questions for market and competitive analysis

pmf:question131.question: What and who are the alternatives currently serving this market and solving the problem? (1.3.1)
pmf:question131.purpose: Competitive landscape mapping and alternative solution identification

subQuestions.1.3.2a: What is the size of the market? (Customers in the addressable market)
subQuestions.1.3.2b: Is the problem growing? (Trends, growth rates, CAGR etc)
subQuestions.1.3.2c: Is the problem general or niche?
subQuestions.1.3.2d: What is the problem frequency? (Continuous, monthly, weekly, daily, resource and time consuming etc)

@graph.rdfs:label: Value Assessment Questions (1.4)
@graph.rdfs:comment: Questions to assess potential value and willingness to pay

pmf:question14.question: What is the potential value of solving the problem? (1.4)
pmf:question14.description: Is the solution valuable and customers willing to pay at a price profitable to your organization?
pmf:question14.citation: Reference: 9 Rules of Pricing and Willingness to Pay methodology from 'Monetizing Innovation' by Madhavan Ramanujam and Georg Tacke
pmf:question14.methodology: Value-based pricing research and willingness-to-pay analysis

pmf:question141.question: What part of the problem remains unresolved or difficult to solve with your product/service in place? (1.4.1)
pmf:question141.purpose: Gap analysis and future roadmap identification

@graph.rdfs:label: Problem Validation
@graph.rdfs:comment: Verification that identified customer problems are significant, urgent, and represent sharpest problems worth solving

pmf:willingnessToPayAnalysis.methodology: 9 Rules of Pricing and Willingness to Pay framework
pmf:willingnessToPayAnalysis.citation: Monetizing Innovation: How Smart Companies Design the Product Around the Price by Madhavan Ramanujam and Georg Tacke
pmf:willingnessToPayAnalysis.approach: Value-based pricing research to determine customer willingness to pay at profitable price points
pmf:willingnessToPayAnalysis.deliverable: WTP Analysis with price sensitivity curves and value perception mapping

pmf:activities: Problem severity assessment using sharpest problems framework
Current solution analysis and workflow mapping
Willingness-to-pay research using Monetizing Innovation methodology
Problem frequency and urgency measurement
Alternative solution evaluation and 3X-5X improvement gap analysis
Latent demand identification and education requirements assessment
pmf:deliverables: Problem Validation Report with sharpest problems ranking
Customer Pain Point Prioritization Matrix (2x2)
Willingness-to-Pay Analysis (citing 9 Rules of Pricing)
Solution Gap Assessment (3X-5X improvement opportunities)
Market Opportunity Sizing with growth trajectory
Latent Demand Education Strategy
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: >=p% of target customers rate problem as critical/urgent AND identify as sharpest problem worth 3X-5X improvement investment

@graph.rdfs:label: Solution Development
@graph.rdfs:comment: Creation and iteration of minimum viable product addressing validated sharpest problems with 3X-5X improvement target
pmf:activities: MVP feature prioritization using sharpest problems matrix
Prototype development focused on 3X-5X improvement areas
User testing and feedback collection
Feature-value mapping against customer workflows
Technical feasibility assessment for core technology layer
Workflow integration and friction minimization design
pmf:deliverables: Minimum Viable Product (MVP) targeting sharpest problems
Feature Specification Document with 3X-5X improvement rationale
User Testing Results validating workflow improvements
Technical Architecture Plan supporting three-layer framework
Product Roadmap prioritized by sharpest problems first
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: MVP solves sharpest customer problems with >=q% satisfaction AND demonstrates 3X-5X improvement over alternatives

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Testing
@graph.rdfs:comment: Systematic measurement of product-market fit using quantitative and qualitative metrics focused on sharpest problems resolution
pmf:activities: Ellis PMF survey deployment with sharpest problems focus
Customer retention analysis by problem severity solved
Usage analytics tracking for core workflow improvements
NPS measurement with 3X-5X improvement validation
Revenue metrics analysis and WTP confirmation
pmf:deliverables: PMF Score Dashboard with sharpest problems correlation
Customer Retention Report by problem type solved
Usage Analytics Summary showing workflow adoption
Net Promoter Score Analysis with improvement attribution
Revenue Performance Metrics validating WTP assumptions
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: Ellis Score >=r% AND Monthly Retention >=s% AND customers validate 3X-5X improvement claims

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Optimization
@graph.rdfs:comment: Continuous improvement of product-market fit through data-driven iterations focused on sharpest problems enhancement
pmf:activities: A/B testing of features addressing sharpest problems
Customer feedback integration and workflow refinement
Pricing optimization using WTP insights from Monetizing Innovation framework
User experience enhancement targeting friction reduction
Market expansion testing for adjacent sharpest problems
pmf:deliverables: A/B Test Results with sharpest problems impact analysis
Feature Impact Analysis on workflow improvements
Pricing Strategy Optimization using 9 Rules framework
UX Improvement Plan for friction minimization
Market Expansion Strategy for related problem domains
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: >=t% improvement in core PMF metrics quarter-over-quarter while maintaining 3X-5X value perception

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Maintenance
@graph.rdfs:comment: Ongoing monitoring and preservation of product-market fit as markets evolve and new sharpest problems emerge
pmf:activities: Continuous market monitoring for emerging sharpest problems
Competitive landscape tracking and 3X-5X advantage preservation
Customer satisfaction surveillance and workflow evolution tracking
Product performance analysis against three-layer framework
Strategic adaptation planning for latent demand opportunities
pmf:deliverables: Market Intelligence Reports identifying new sharpest problems
Competitive Analysis Updates maintaining 3X-5X advantage
Customer Health Scorecards tracking workflow satisfaction
Performance Trend Analysis across three-layer framework
Strategic Adaptation Recommendations for emerging opportunities
@graph.pmf:successCriteria: PMF metrics maintained >=u months with <v% degradation while adapting to evolving sharpest problems

@graph.rdfs:label: Organization
@graph.rdfs:comment: Business entity implementing PMF processes

@graph.rdfs:label: Product
@graph.rdfs:comment: Software, service, or solution being optimized for market fit

@graph.rdfs:label: Value Proposition
@graph.rdfs:comment: Unique value delivered to customers solving their core problems

@graph.rdfs:label: Customer Segment
@graph.rdfs:comment: Specific group of customers with common characteristics and needs

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Metric
@graph.rdfs:comment: Quantitative measurement of product-market fit strength

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Assessment
@graph.rdfs:comment: Systematic evaluation of current product-market fit status

@graph.rdfs:label: Experiment Cycle
@graph.rdfs:comment: Hypothesis-driven testing cycle for PMF optimization

@graph.rdfs:label: Has Process
@graph.rdfs:comment: Organization implements PMF process

@graph.rdfs:label: Has Product
@graph.rdfs:comment: Organization has product in PMF process

@graph.rdfs:label: Has Value Proposition
@graph.rdfs:comment: Product delivers value proposition

@graph.rdfs:label: Targets Segment
@graph.rdfs:comment: Value proposition targets customer segment

@graph.rdfs:label: Measured By
@graph.rdfs:comment: PMF process measured by specific metrics

@graph.rdfs:label: Assessed By
@graph.rdfs:comment: Product PMF assessed by assessment process

@graph.rdfs:label: Optimized Through
@graph.rdfs:comment: PMF optimized through experiment cycles

@graph.rdfs:label: Aligns With
@graph.rdfs:comment: PMF process aligns with organizational strategy/OKRs

@graph.rdfs:label: Has Stage
@graph.rdfs:comment: Current stage in PMF process

@graph.rdfs:label: PMF Score
@graph.rdfs:comment: Quantitative PMF measurement score

@graph.rdfs:label: Assessment Date
@graph.rdfs:comment: Date of PMF assessment

@graph.rdfs:label: Confidence Level
@graph.rdfs:comment: Statistical confidence in PMF measurement

@graph.rdfs:label: Implementation Timeline
@graph.rdfs:comment: Expected timeline for PMF process implementation

pmf:processWorkflow.schema:name: BAIV PMF Process Workflow with Sharpest Problems Framework
pmf:processWorkflow.schema:description: Complete workflow for achieving and maintaining product-market fit using three-layer execution framework

schema:workflowSteps.schema:name: Customer Discovery
schema:workflowSteps.schema:description: Identify and validate target customer segments using three-layer framework and sharpest problems prioritization
pmf:deliverables: ICP Document with sharpest problems focus
Customer Journey Maps with workflow friction analysis
Market Analysis Report (size, growth, frequency)
Competitive Alternatives Assessment (1.3.1)
Sharpest Problems Matrix (2x2: Easy/Hard vs Low/Sharp)
3X-5X Improvement Opportunities Map
schema:workflowSteps.pmf:successCriteria: >=n validated interviews identifying sharpest problems and 3X-5X improvement potential

schema:workflowSteps.schema:name: Problem Validation
schema:workflowSteps.schema:description: Confirm customer problems are sharpest problems worth 3X-5X investment using WTP methodology
pmf:keyActivities: Apply 9 Rules of Pricing methodology from Monetizing Innovation
Conduct Willingness-to-Pay research for sharpest problems
Assess latent demand and education requirements
Confirm problem frequency and workflow impact
pmf:deliverables: Problem Validation Report ranking sharpest problems
WTP Analysis citing Monetizing Innovation framework
3X-5X Improvement Gap Analysis vs current solutions
Latent Demand Assessment and education strategy
Market Opportunity Sizing with CAGR projections
schema:workflowSteps.pmf:successCriteria: >=p% rate as critical/urgent sharpest problem + confirmed WTP at profitable pricing

schema:workflowSteps.schema:name: Solution Development
schema:workflowSteps.schema:description: Build MVP targeting sharpest problems with 3X-5X improvement over alternatives
pmf:keyActivities: Prioritize features using Sharpest Problems First framework
Design for 3X-5X workflow improvement vs alternatives
Position as 'can't live without it' solution
pmf:deliverables: MVP solving sharpest problems with minimal friction
Feature Specs with 3X-5X improvement rationale
User Testing Results validating workflow transformation
Technical Architecture supporting three-layer framework
schema:workflowSteps.pmf:successCriteria: MVP demonstrates 3X-5X improvement with >=q% customer satisfaction

schema:workflowSteps.schema:name: PMF Testing
schema:workflowSteps.schema:description: Measure product-market fit focusing on sharpest problems resolution and 3X-5X value delivery
pmf:keyActivities: Deploy Ellis PMF surveys with sharpest problems context
Track retention by problem severity solved
Measure workflow adoption and friction reduction
Confirm WTP assumptions from Monetizing Innovation framework
pmf:deliverables: PMF Score Dashboard (>=r% target)
Retention Analysis by sharpest problems solved
3X-5X Improvement Validation Report
Workflow Adoption and Friction Metrics

schema:workflowSteps.schema:name: PMF Optimization
schema:workflowSteps.schema:description: Iteratively improve PMF while maintaining 3X-5X value advantage
pmf:keyActivities: A/B test features impacting sharpest problems
Enhance workflows for greater friction reduction
pmf:deliverables: A/B Test Results with sharpest problems impact
Pricing Optimization using WTP insights
Workflow Enhancement Plans
Adjacent Market Opportunities Assessment

schema:workflowSteps.schema:name: PMF Maintenance
schema:workflowSteps.schema:description: Monitor and preserve PMF as markets evolve and new sharpest problems emerge
pmf:keyActivities: Assess workflow evolution and new friction points
Adapt three-layer framework to market changes
pmf:deliverables: Market Intelligence on emerging sharpest problems
Competitive Advantage Preservation Strategy
Workflow Evolution Tracking Reports
Strategic Adaptation Recommendations
schema:workflowSteps.pmf:successCriteria: PMF metrics maintained >=u months adapting to evolving problems

organizationalStrategy.vision: PMF process supports long-term organizational vision
organizationalStrategy.mission: Customer-centric approach aligns with mission delivery
organizationalStrategy.values: Data-driven decision making reflects organizational values

okrFramework.objectives: PMF metrics become key organizational objectives
okrFramework.keyResults: Specific PMF scores and retention rates as measurable results
okrFramework.alignment: PMF OKRs cascade from company to team to individual level

productDevelopment.roadmap: PMF insights directly inform product roadmap prioritization
productDevelopment.features: Customer feedback drives feature development decisions
productDevelopment.releases: PMF metrics gate major product releases

marketingStrategy.messaging: Customer language from PMF research shapes messaging
marketingStrategy.channels: PMF data identifies most effective customer acquisition channels

pmf:continuousAssessment.frequency: Monthly PMF metric collection and quarterly deep assessment

automation.dataCollection: Automated customer surveys and usage analytics
automation.alerting: Real-time alerts for PMF metric threshold breaches
automation.reporting: Automated PMF dashboard updates and trend analysis

qualitative.teamConfidence: Internal team confidence in product direction
